messages.text: Victor Romero Sr. The orange belt is observed to be loose and rubbing against the equipment structure, causing an accumulation of dust or product residue in that area. It is recommended to adjust the belt and clean away the accumulated residue to prevent contamination and equipment wear.
Maintenance is coming over to help with that
Please make sure we have 10 in each pouch. Minimum is 10 target is 10 and max is 11. Victor Romero Sr.
Victor Romero Sr. were there any differences between your count and IMS?
There was a 41155 bag count with IMS and we have 31441 pouches  Curt Willis Justin Garner
Erandy Garcilazo Ceja can you please have 10169 HER almonds temper sheet and decorator temper sheet on Sdrive? Different shifts are using different temper sheets for this. Thank you
Curt Willis, we have 334,741 available pouches for both HERs orders and need 294,840 to complete both HERs orders.
Charity Young we need clarification on this section. We have already out in the caramel, pre-piece and nuts. We are not sure what you are asking here?
I believe that’s the caramel. Let me verify with Candon.
10 pieces and 3 almonds
10 pieces and 3 almonds
Matrix 7 is not gripping the bag. Maintenance adjusted the height of the suction cups and also replaced them. Product has accumulated; we will pause the line to process the accumulated product.
Matrix 6 isn't opening the Pouches Ray is here working on the Matrix.
10 pieces and 3 almonds

Gripper arms on MM6 were going in too soon after pouch opening. Ray and Chris adjusted physical cam. Also, the bottom pouch tapper was turned on to help settle product. Justin Garner it seems to work a lot better.

Scrap calculation - Almomd cluster - pre enrober mid shift 
Pre piece scrap= 34 lb 
Justin Garner Victor Romero Sr.

Operators are reporting that some pouches are sealed. They are going through them before loading

Can you please let me know if we are seeing a lot of pouches sealed? Can you  also please give me an estimated quantity and if it's from the same lot or different lots? I'd like us to take this back to the pouch supplier and let them know we've been seeing these issues.
10 pieces and 4 almonds
Janell Scharrer only 20 pouches were found so far that were sealed
thank you. let me know if we are seeing a large amount sealed

inspecting the rollers and scrapers
Tunnel 2 is in good condition and has no caramel buildup.
Tunnel 3 in good condition, with no salt accumulation.

Scrap calculation - Almomd cluster - pre enrober end of shift

Nuts fell on Floor= 7 lb 
Justin Garner Victor Romero Sr.
End of 1 shift

We’ve paused the line due to Tunnel 3 slipping/stopping due to salt accumulation. 

The compressed air for the “salt brush” at the end of tunnel 3 was turned off. 

We’ve cleared the salt underneath the belt on tunnel 3, and are removing the rollers and scrapers to clear the accumulation.
We’ve started the line again.
10 bins of accumulation at SOS
Shift 3
We are working with QA trying to change the date on the boxe printer but they have been unable to figure it out
There are almost 6 pallets snd counting of boxes without the date. We will rework them during the reheat.
They have figured it out and changed it to the correct.
Down for reheat

The reheat is complete. There is no hard chocolate remaining under the decorator. We did have to cut a bit of chocolate off, let it fall down and move it to be melted by the running chocolate as well as do 20 minutes of a second reheat.
We are getting into temper

Matrix 8
Matrix 7

The weight is too high—well outside the product specification standards—so we need to make adjustments to improve it; the operator has been notified and we are awaiting improvement.TruffleLine Enrober

Organoleptic test check update 


 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications.

Tempering chart test check update


The temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.

Weight test check update after the adjustments


The weight looks good; we are within the parameters required by the product specifications.

Flow-wrap check update 


The flow-wrap packaging is coming out in perfect condition; the seals are intact, and the corners are well-sealed. The "best if used by" print on the flow-wrap is legible and clear

Weight test check update 


The weight looks good; we are within the parameters required by the product specifications.

Organoleptic test check update 


 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications.

Tempering chart test check update


 


The temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.

The rails for Cabana 2 were improperly installed.The rails have letters and numbers for positioning; R3, R4, L4 y L3 wrong position were in another.

The tempermeter was received with a broken printer cover. It does not print the paper or display the temper result. Maintenance is currently checking it. In the meantime, we will use the tempermeter from ML4.
A box of Grahams was put into hold, the box is broken and possibly the bag too, perhaps it was caused for the forklift forks. I attach photos

Goodmorning! I followed up with the 3rd shift FL operator & went over the importance of inspecting pallets when offloading/staging. 

It seems this pallet arrived damaged but the 3rd shift operator did not notice when offloading or staging for grinding. Alan Todd -Hervin Herrera-Gustavo Rodas
Thanks Joey Michael Dudney

Gustavo Rodas, Did anyone let you know that the compressed air hose reel was operational?
If they did, was it helpful?
No, thanks for letting me know and for getting this done.
QA is getting the sample for the second batch cooked at 238

Minimal, gentle stirring is acceptable to ensure that the sample cup has a homogenous temperature so ideally right before taking a viscosity reading. Stirring cannot be used to lower the sample temperature.

Italu Martinez. Can you make a written test procedure for caramel viscosity testing 

Test at this temp, don’t stir until desired temp, this many seconds gently stirring before testing, etc

There is one. 
Candon Watt

These are the 2 jars I got at 4:10 with the cook temp of 237.5 




Curt Willis, Italu Martinez
If this first result is not favorable, we proceed to take a second sample.
If this first result is not favorable, we proceed to take a second sample.
Curt Willis
What’s the temperature?
Going up in cooking temp is going to change the testing temp.
The cooking temperature is 238° now.
We will start up
Italu Martinez. You make the call if you want to stay at this cook temp or go up a little more based on results.

Dakota Adair. I’m making a decision to run.   

continue to test viscosity every 30 minutes and post pictures of finished product every 30 minutes please
2nd result; a 3rd sample will be taken.
Aw result
Line is running now. Dakota Adair Curt Willis

Make sure we take a measurement of the final piece once it gets in back 


Dakota Adair like we talked about please post pictures of the first jar and then every hour after that
Measure 10 pieces per check. We’ll be reviewing the data tomorrow.
In the pre-piece, the caramel has a more substantial consistency.
Final piece
What are the dimensions?
Go up 1 degree pls. Let's keep a close eye on the texture. At this point we've gone up 3.5 degrees and based on earlier results before the changes, we were almost on target.
Dakota Adair William Beattie
Looks in spec 👍.    Look good to you Italu!
We have 24 pieces in the jars.
What are the current avgs for height and diameter?
Posted above

Both kettles are currently cooking at 238°. The operator says we must wait until those batches finish cooking before we can increase the temperature to 239°. I will post a photo/update when it's done. Dakota Adair Curt Willis Italu Martinez
Ok
Italu Martinez are you sure you want to go up 1 more degree?  Or do you want to wait?
I only see two measurements taken for each of the two clusters. Avg of 10 is needed for height and diameter
Yes, I'll do them; I just wanted to be sure that everything is okay right up to the end.Italu Martinez

I can only speak on the data that has been provided and based on the last viscosity reading, it seems like we need to go up.  
Once the next batch at 238 comes out, lets test and evaluate.
Pin intact
MD rollers
William Beattie Alfonsina Gil.  Are we filing out the board we talked about
Please include the average height and width on the board we need the average of 10 pieces every 30 minutes.

Right now the 10-piece average is 47.4mm diameter, and 14mm height. I will have the team record this information and include it on the whiteboard with the piece weights. Dakota Adair  Curt Willis Charity Young
Higher viscosity caramel... as in, more watery. 😬 That, or we increase the caramel deposition volume, but at that point we'd be making the piece-weights out of spec.
Italu Martinez what do you think about diameter at 47.4mm? Keep it there?

Charity Young, my apologies, this is my first time seeing these instructions. We're taking another measurement now, and I believe the diameter is within this range. Italu Martinez Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Two samples with 238, It continue low
It's within range so it's fine.
What's the water activity?
No that’s was my mistake I forgot to pass that information along due to worrying about the front end I’ll have them make one now.
Shall we continue cooking at 238°? Or increase to 239°? Italu Martinez
The diameter should not be less than 50mm what can be done to improve this?
There is a thick layer of liquid in the depositor. We are stopping the line so that we can empty the depositor and refill is with fresh caramel. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
I’ll fix safety chain.
Charity Young. Not more than 50.
No more than 50mm. Current diameter and height are within range and acceptable.
Is that oil? Water?
Try and reduce the level of the caramel.  When there is too much the agitation because of a higher level the caramel will separate from the butter
Oil / butter

This is concerning. Water activity should not be this high. 
Please keep an eye out for any condensation or areas where water can be introduced.
Curt Willis Italu Martinez are we going up to 239 or staying at 238
Let's take another sample
Caleb York, we have a problem with the capper. Can you help please?
Stay at 238 unless Italu Martinez says to change please
Can someone post a picture of the current product and of the pieces from earlier
Was walking by and saw they were having issues with the Capper. I think I found the problem.

We have
taken measurements of the parts in two stages; these are the results.
We are increasing it on the current batch we are making
Let's do 238.5 pls. Earlier today we were at .40 & .43 and now it's at .60. For water activity this is a significant jump and more so with a temp increase.
I observe the watery caramel
Thanks angelica
I see traces of oil
Please take water activity of what it is in the hopper if you haven't already

This is from 7pm 


This is from 8pm 


This is from 9pm 


Italu Martinez, Curt Willis I’ve only witnessed one or two pieces having pinhole caramel leaks
Is it "watery" bc of the oil or bc we think water is somehow being introduced in it?
We have one in the machine, I can have one with the caramel now too.
In my opinion, we should drain the caramel again, as it seems to have too much oil.
I’m going to say this again.  The hopper level is too full causing the caramel to separate.
I have the sample I just took in the machine.
In the pre-piece stage, we have good caramel consistency, despite the excess oil in the caramel reservoir.
Part of the issue also is that the lid retainer plate is quite worn, which is jamming the lids occasionally
That looks better. 👍
Italu Martinez
Viscosity ,Wa,Brix
We lowered the level of the caramel in the depositor. Curt Willis
This is the result with the caramel and oil in the hopper
Thank you!
I have updated safety chain Piece Check form to make the width 50mm MAX.  - please refresh for your next checks.

TEAM - After talking with Italu Martinez we agree that maybe the system needs to actualize to the adjustments that have been made. The viscosity and aW look ok as of the last checks. 
Please keep running as normal. We'll keep evaluating the results and collect data. 
The consistency of the caramel looks good from the pictures. 
Thanks to ALL for the efforts and attention to the issues!

Angelica Perez will communicate to Ebrima Dibba that we will take viscosity and aW checks from the pipe feeding the caramel depositor. (This is what QA have been doing and gives the most accurate results.)
Also that the important factor is the physical organoleptic checks of the finished piece have consistent caramel. Firm and not thin when you break them apart. Thanks to all!
I will!

Shawn Jewkes I not sure if you want to look into the recipe and update the CL. All three shifts are running varied settings. Last night one of the big issues with the DT was the high limit weight was changed from 25 to 45.
We should be following the CL. Why are shifts just changing things?
We definitely don't want to adjust it to accept 45 grams over target...

I agree I am just reviewing the CL documents this is what I am seeing. 
Kim walked me through high limit setpoint and that it got changed at some point.
CL set points are being changed to keep the machine running. Due to all the issues. Maybe you can take a look at this.
We are having issues with buckets #7 and #10 not opening, maintenance has been called.
Still having this issue, but it's better now.

Are they trying to cycle but can't, or are they not trying to cycle at all? Are they overfilling? I noticed yesterday that when they were significantly overfull, the buckets don't cycle so as not to overload bags. If too much product is getting onto the feed tray, it can result in unintentional overfill in the buckets.
They did cycle it, and I coached the EE's that are feeding the conveyors with product as well, it seems to be better now.

Pouch check update


The pouch has a total of 34 pieces (8 pieces for Dark, Toffee, and Milk each, and 10 pieces for Caramel). The LOT number and "Best If Used By" date are accurate and legible.
Sensor is not functioning correctly and stopping the product flow
I think one of the issues on why the sensor isn’t stopping the feed is because where it’s positioned chocolate drops on it and knocks it out of the correct position

I adjusted the position of the sensors and have been watching it for the past 20 mins and haven’t noticed any issues regarding it stopping accordingly 

They are staying full but don’t fill too much
We got an printer fault and needed to be cleaned when removing the head we found it like this
There’s heavy ink build up on the right side

Another issue we were having was the buckets kept over flowing even with the curtains at lowest position while looking at the CL I noticed MVF time and amp was higher than they should be so I lowered them within range it happens occasionally but as often now
Nice!
